source.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

claim_chain: Readout inversion is fundamental to quantitative image formation but has not been considered in functional superresolution imaging
FINICI is not restricted to fluctuating output and should suppress other characteristics inherent to these fluorescent proteins
The sensitivity of FINICI matches and can exceed that of FRET
FINICI can reveal signaling dynamics at superresolution
The superresolution visualization reported here will complement tools such as optogenetics to confirm the precise spatiotemporal control of active Src
Our imaging supports the literature and directly showed how cGMP compartmentalization is stimulation dependent

typical_pattern: Opening statement of principle or gap
Technical description of mechanism or approach
Comparison to existing methods or literature
Presentation of key findings with specific examples

likely_priorities: Validation of biosensor responses with appropriate controls
Quantitative comparison to established methods like FRET
likely_attacks: Complexity introduced by photoswitching phase in fast protocols
Reliability concerns with basal-state minimization using long linkers
Need for optimization of switch orientation relative to RFP
Expression level requirements for adequate signal-to-noise
reviewer_preference_profile.inference_basis: The paper explicitly addresses validation steps: 'Each FINICI biosensor reported here was optimized over a range of excitation laser intensities with appropriate controls to validate responses.' It acknowledges temporal resolution limitations: 'The reported 51-s temporal resolution was limited here by resource/throughput.' It discusses photoswitching complexities: 'When using the fast protocol for FINICI-SrcAR, the photoswitching phase may give complexities.' It notes optimization needs: 'To further enhance a given FINICI biosensor, optimizing the switch orientation relative to the RFP will likely give the best return.' It mentions reliability issues: 'attempts at basal-state minimization with a long linker made these biosensors less reliable in FINICI readout.' It addresses expression concerns: 'very high expression is unnecessary and to aid signal-to-noise, cells with poor expression of the biosensor should be avoided.'

we believe this perspective hints at an upstream-centric nature of cGMP signaling
directly showed how cGMP compartmentalization is stimulation dependent

phrase_evidence.phrase: Readout inversion is fundamental to quantitative image formation but has not been considered in functional superresolution imaging
We break this barrier and rationally develop a genetically encoded principle
As a principle, FINICI is not restricted to fluctuating output
the sensitivity of FINICI matches and can exceed that of FRET
It is genetically encodable, does not require the introduction of exogenous dyes or enzymes
Data showed that FINICI can reveal signaling dynamics at superresolution
We have outlined and shown the steps for adopting new inverse switches
Each FINICI biosensor reported here was optimized over a range of excitation laser intensities with appropriate controls to validate responses
The reported 51-s temporal resolution was limited here by resource/throughput
attempts at basal-state minimization with a long linker made these biosensors less reliable in FINICI readout
To further enhance a given FINICI biosensor, optimizing the switch orientation relative to the RFP will likely give the best return
Our imaging supports the literature and directly showed how cGMP compartmentalization is stimulation dependent
Our results suggested that SNO-stimulated cGMP drives less compartment weakening
we believe this perspective hints at an upstream-centric nature of cGMP signaling
The superresolution visualization reported here will complement tools such as optogenetics
FINICI-SykAR will help visualize and differentiate between these models
normalized pcSOFI images of live HeLa cells expressing WT raft-targeted biosensor before/after huEGF stimulation
Domain structure of FINICI-cGi and resultant whole cell averaged fluorescence intensity traces
Fast FINICI-cGi kymographs directly captured compartment weakening
The flexibilities introduced here to superresolution activity imaging
Where applicable, appropriate filtering is applied to isolate the fluctuation phase containing statistical information
While the normalized pcSOFI response is independent of biosensor expression
very high expression is unnecessary and to aid signal-to-noise, cells with poor expression of the biosensor should be avoided
dynamic imaging remains possible by tuning a higher excitation intensity to optimally cycle the biosensors
This is a significant obstacle for understanding the ways cells organize their signaling via nanodomains and compartments

verification_status.notes: All extracted phrases are literal substrings from the input text. No external references, author names, or invented numbers were included. The profile is based solely on the provided abstract, discussion, and figure legends.
